title: dianthus caespitosus subsp. pectinatus
intro: Dianthus caespitosus subsp. pectinatus, also known as the alpine pink or fragrant pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its pink to white flowers and small, succulent leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Dianthus caespitosus subsp. pectinatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
